18: // beadsCache provides TTL-based caching for beads data to prevent excessive
19: // bd process spawning when the dashboard polls /api/agents frequently.
20: // Without caching, each request can spawn 20+ concurrent bd processes for 600+ workspaces.
21: type beadsCache struct {
22: 	mu sync.RWMutex
23: 
24: 	// Cached data
25: 	openIssues map[string]*verify.Issue
26: 	allIssues  map[string]*verify.Issue
27: 	comments   map[string][]beads.Comment
28: 
29: 	// Cache metadata
30: 	openIssuesFetchedAt         time.Time
31: 	allIssuesFetchedAt          time.Time
32: 	allIssuesFetchedFor         []string // Track which beads IDs were fetched
33: 	allIssuesFetchedProjectDirs map[string]string
34: 	commentsFetchedAt           time.Time
35: 	commentsFetchedFor          []string // Track which beads IDs were fetched
36: 	commentsFetchedProjectDirs  map[string]string
37: 
38: 	// TTL configuration
39: 	openIssuesTTL time.Duration
40: 	allIssuesTTL  time.Duration
41: 	commentsTTL   time.Duration
42: }
43: 
44: // globalWorkspaceCache provides TTL-based caching for workspace metadata.
45: // Without caching, each /api/agents request scans 600+ SPAWN_CONTEXT.md files.
46: type globalWorkspaceCacheType struct {
47: 	mu sync.RWMutex
48: 
49: 	// Cached data
50: 	cache *workspaceCache
51: 
52: 	// Cache metadata
53: 	fetchedAt   time.Time
54: 	ttl         time.Duration
55: 	projectDirs []string // Track which project dirs the cache was built with
56: }
57: 
58: // Global workspace cache
59: var globalWorkspaceCacheInstance = &globalWorkspaceCacheType{
60: 	ttl: 10 * time.Second, // Shorter TTL improves freshness during high spawn activity
61: }
62: 
63: // getCachedWorkspace returns cached workspace data or builds fresh if stale.
64: // Rebuilds cache if:
65: // 1. Cache is nil (never built or invalidated)
66: // 2. Cache TTL expired
67: // 3. Project directories have changed (new projects registered)
68: func (c *globalWorkspaceCacheType) getCachedWorkspace(projectDirs []string) *workspaceCache {
69: 	c.mu.RLock()
70: 	cacheValid := c.cache != nil && time.Since(c.fetchedAt) < c.ttl
71: 	dirsMatch := projectDirsMatch(c.projectDirs, projectDirs)
72: 	if cacheValid && dirsMatch {
73: 		result := c.cache
74: 		c.mu.RUnlock()
75: 		return result
76: 	}
77: 	c.mu.RUnlock()
78: 
79: 	// Build fresh workspace cache
80: 	wsCache := buildMultiProjectWorkspaceCache(projectDirs)
81: 
82: 	c.mu.Lock()
83: 	c.cache = wsCache
84: 	c.fetchedAt = time.Now()
85: 	c.projectDirs = projectDirs // Store the project dirs this cache was built with
86: 	c.mu.Unlock()
87: 
88: 	return wsCache
89: }
90: 
91: // projectDirsMatch checks if two slices of project directories contain the same entries.
92: // Order doesn't matter, but all entries must match.
93: func projectDirsMatch(a, b []string) bool {
94: 	if len(a) != len(b) {
95: 		return false
96: 	}
97: 	// Create a set from a
98: 	aSet := make(map[string]bool, len(a))
99: 	for _, dir := range a {
100: 		aSet[dir] = true
101: 	}
102: 	// Check all entries in b are in a
103: 	for _, dir := range b {
104: 		if !aSet[dir] {
105: 			return false
106: 		}
107: 	}
108: 	return true
109: }
110: 
111: // Default TTLs for cached data
112: // These TTLs balance freshness with performance. With 600+ sessions, even cached
113: // data fetches are expensive when TTL expires. Longer TTLs reduce fetch frequency.
114: // Use /api/cache/invalidate to force refresh when needed (e.g., after orch complete).
115: const (
116: 	defaultOpenIssuesTTL = 30 * time.Second // Open issues change infrequently
117: 	defaultAllIssuesTTL  = 60 * time.Second // Closed issues change even less
118: 	defaultCommentsTTL   = 15 * time.Second // Comments change more often (phase updates)
119: )
120: 
121: // invalidate clears all cached data, forcing fresh fetches on next request.
122: // This is called when agents complete to ensure the dashboard shows current status.
123: func (c *beadsCache) invalidate() {
124: 	c.mu.Lock()
125: 	defer c.mu.Unlock()
126: 
127: 	// Reset all cached data
128: 	c.openIssues = make(map[string]*verify.Issue)
129: 	c.allIssues = make(map[string]*verify.Issue)
130: 	c.comments = make(map[string][]beads.Comment)
131: 
132: 	// Reset timestamps to force refetch
133: 	c.openIssuesFetchedAt = time.Time{}
134: 	c.allIssuesFetchedAt = time.Time{}
135: 	c.commentsFetchedAt = time.Time{}
136: 	c.allIssuesFetchedFor = nil
137: 	c.allIssuesFetchedProjectDirs = nil
138: 	c.commentsFetchedFor = nil
139: 	c.commentsFetchedProjectDirs = nil
140: }
141: 
142: // invalidate clears the cached workspace data, forcing a fresh scan on next request.
143: // This is called when agents complete to ensure the dashboard shows current status.
144: func (c *globalWorkspaceCacheType) invalidate() {
145: 	c.mu.Lock()
146: 	defer c.mu.Unlock()
147: 
148: 	c.cache = nil
149: 	c.fetchedAt = time.Time{}
150: 	c.projectDirs = nil
151: }
152: 
153: // Global beads cache instance, initialized in runServe
154: var globalBeadsCache *beadsCache
155: 
156: var getIssuesBatch = verify.GetIssuesBatch
157: var getCommentsBatchWithProjectDirs = verify.GetCommentsBatchWithProjectDirs
158: 
159: // newBeadsCache creates a new beads cache with default TTLs.
160: func newBeadsCache() *beadsCache {
161: 	return &beadsCache{
162: 		openIssues:    make(map[string]*verify.Issue),
163: 		allIssues:     make(map[string]*verify.Issue),
164: 		comments:      make(map[string][]beads.Comment),
165: 		openIssuesTTL: defaultOpenIssuesTTL,
166: 		allIssuesTTL:  defaultAllIssuesTTL,
167: 		commentsTTL:   defaultCommentsTTL,
168: 	}
169: }
170: 
171: // getOpenIssues returns cached open issues or fetches fresh data if cache is stale.
172: func (c *beadsCache) getOpenIssues() (map[string]*verify.Issue, error) {
173: 	c.mu.RLock()
174: 	if time.Since(c.openIssuesFetchedAt) < c.openIssuesTTL && len(c.openIssues) > 0 {
175: 		result := c.openIssues
176: 		c.mu.RUnlock()
177: 		return result, nil
178: 	}
179: 	c.mu.RUnlock()
180: 
181: 	// Fetch fresh data
182: 	issues, err := verify.ListOpenIssues()
183: 	if err != nil {
184: 		return nil, err
185: 	}
186: 
187: 	c.mu.Lock()
188: 	c.openIssues = issues
189: 	c.openIssuesFetchedAt = time.Now()
190: 	c.mu.Unlock()
191: 
192: 	return issues, nil
193: }
194: 
195: // getAllIssues returns cached issues or fetches fresh data if cache is stale.
196: // The beadsIDs parameter specifies which issues to fetch. If the cached set
197: // matches the requested set and is not expired, returns cached data.
198: // projectDirs provides beadsID -> projectDir mappings for cross-project lookups.
199: func (c *beadsCache) getAllIssues(beadsIDs []string, projectDirs map[string]string) (map[string]*verify.Issue, error) {
200: 	c.mu.RLock()
201: 	if time.Since(c.allIssuesFetchedAt) < c.allIssuesTTL &&
202: 		c.containsAllIDs(c.allIssuesFetchedFor, beadsIDs) &&
203: 		c.projectDirsMatchForIDs(c.allIssuesFetchedProjectDirs, projectDirs, beadsIDs) {
204: 		result := c.allIssues
205: 		c.mu.RUnlock()
206: 		return result, nil

208: 	c.mu.RUnlock()
209:

212: 	if err != nil {
213: 		return nil, err

215:

217: 	c.allIssues = issues
218: 	c.allIssuesFetchedAt = time.Now()
219: 	c.allIssuesFetchedFor = beadsIDs
220: 	c.allIssuesFetchedProjectDirs = cloneProjectDirsForIDs(projectDirs, beadsIDs)
221: 	c.mu.Unlock()
222: 
223: 	return issues, nil
224: }
225: 
226: // getComments returns cached comments or fetches fresh data if cache is stale.
227: // The beadsIDs and projectDirs parameters specify which comments to fetch.
228: func (c *beadsCache) getComments(beadsIDs []string, projectDirs map[string]string) map[string][]beads.Comment {
229: 	c.mu.RLock()
230: 	if time.Since(c.commentsFetchedAt) < c.commentsTTL &&
231: 		c.containsAllIDs(c.commentsFetchedFor, beadsIDs) &&
232: 		c.projectDirsMatchForIDs(c.commentsFetchedProjectDirs, projectDirs, beadsIDs) {
233: 		result := c.comments
234: 		c.mu.RUnlock()

236: 	}
237: 	c.mu.RUnlock()
238: 
239: 	// Fetch fresh data

241: 
242: 	c.mu.Lock()
243: 	c.comments = comments
244: 	c.commentsFetchedAt = time.Now()

246: 	c.commentsFetchedProjectDirs = cloneProjectDirsForIDs(projectDirs, beadsIDs)
247: 	c.mu.Unlock()
248: 
249: 	return comments
250: }

Tests expecting agents without workspace will be updated to reflect this filtering change.

328: // listSessionsAcrossProjects queries OpenCode for sessions across all known projects.
329: // OpenCode scopes session listing by the x-opencode-directory header, so querying
330: // with "" only returns sessions for the server's default project. Cross-project agents
331: // (e.g., price-watch agents spawned from orch-go) are invisible without this.
332: // Fix: query the default project + all registered kb projects, then deduplicate by session ID.
333: func listSessionsAcrossProjects(client *opencode.Client, currentProjectDir string) ([]opencode.Session, error) {
334: 	// Start with default query (no directory header = server's default project)
335: 	sessions, err := client.ListSessions("")
336: 	if err != nil {
337: 		return nil, err
338: 	}
339: 
340: 	// Build set of session IDs we already have
341: 	seen := make(map[string]bool, len(sessions))
342: 	for _, s := range sessions {
343: 		seen[s.ID] = true
344: 	}
345: 
346: 	// Query each registered kb project for its sessions
347: 	for _, projectDir := range getKBProjectsFn() {
348: 		// Skip current project (already covered by default query)
349: 		if filepath.Clean(projectDir) == filepath.Clean(currentProjectDir) {

**Confirming test server handles session path and planning new test**

The test server expects `/session/` with trailing slash, so calls to `/session` return 404 but are tolerated by the code logging a warning and proceeding with no sessions. This means a new test can use the existing test server setup and handle potential 404s safely. I'm ready to create the new test for handling agents including the review queue label.

**Implementing test setup and patch for label inclusion**

I'm setting up environment like existing tests with globals and a temp project directory but no workspace. The test uses issue lists including the "daemon:ready-review" label and expects handleAgents to process this correctly. Next, I'll implement the patch to ensure the label is included so the test passes.

+verification:

+
+  manual_steps:
+    - description: 'Reproduce dead-agent flood on existing server (pre-fix)'
+      command: 'curl -sk https://localhost:3348/api/agents | python3 -c "import json,sys; data=json.load(sys.stdin); print(\"agents\", len(data)); print(\"dead\", sum(1 for a in data if a.get(\"status\")==\"dead\"))"'
+      result: 'agents 283; dead 240'
+    - description: 'Verify filtered agents on patched server'
+      command: 'go run -ldflags "-X main.sourceDir=/Users/dylanconlin/Documents/personal/orch-go" ./cmd/orch serve --port 3349 ...'
+      result: 'agents 25; dead 3'
+
+deliverables:
+  - file: 'cmd/orch/serve_agents_handlers.go'
+    change: 'Filter beads-first issues to those with workspace/session/daemon label evidence'

+
+notes:
